15.reason: After creating a new node, the function should return immediately. This line is executed because there is no 'else' before it, causing the same value to be inserted again into the left subtree and then into the right subtree (since tem is not less than the newly assigned data, the else branch runs). This results in duplicate nodes and incorrect BST structure.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Missing else before if; when root is not NULL, this if executes unconditionally after the previous if block, causing incorrect logic for duplicate or equal values.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing else before if; when root is not NULL, this if is always executed, but if root was just created (NULL case), root->data is set, but then this if checks tem < root->data, which may cause incorrect insertion logic. Should be "else if (tem < root->data)".
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  After creating a new node, the function should return immediately. This line is executed because there is no 'else' before it, causing the same value to be inserted again into the left subtree and then into the right subtree (since tem is not less than the newly assigned data, the else branch runs). This results in duplicate nodes and incorrect BST structure.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Missing else: when root is just created, this if is still executed, leading to duplicate insertion and incorrect tree structure.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  Missing 'else' causes fall-through after node allocation, leading to infinite recursion when inserting values.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  Missing 'else' keyword. After allocating a new node, the condition 'tem < root->data' evaluates to false, causing execution to fall through to the 'else' block. This results in infinite recursion as the same value is repeatedly inserted into the right child.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Missing 'else' before this 'if'; causes incorrect insertion logic when root is not NULL and tem >= root->data, leading to unintended double insertion or undefined behavior]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Missing 'else' before this 'if'; causes incorrect insertion logic when root is not NULL and tem >= root->data, leading to unintended overwriting or undefined behavior]

However, the structure is fine functionally, but the missing else is not an error here because of the return. The real issue is in insert function.
